name: Indian Councils Act 1909 (Morley–Minto Reforms)
era: Crown Rule — Constitutional Frameworks of British India (1858–1947)
key_provisions: Separate electorates and reserved seats for Muslims
Elected non-official majorities in provincial councils
First Indian admitted to the Viceroy's Executive Council (S.P. Sinha)
Members could move resolutions and ask supplementary questions
demise: Absorbed into the Government of India Act 1919; separate electorates persisted to 1947 and shaped the two-nation constitutional argument.
